overarchingQuestion: What are trees? How do trees grow, change, and help the world around them?

weeklyInvestigationQuestions.week1: What is a tree? What parts does a tree have?
weeklyInvestigationQuestions.week2: What do trees need to grow? How do trees help us?
weeklyInvestigationQuestions.week3: How do trees change? What lives in and around trees?
weeklyInvestigationQuestions.week4: How do people care for trees? How can we be tree helpers?
weeklyInvestigationQuestions.week5_optional: What have we learned about trees? How can we share what we know?

anchorMaterials: How to Describe a Tree chart (built Day 1, added to throughout)
Class Tree Journal (shared documentation book, added to daily)
Wonder Wall (questions children generate, displayed all month)
Kindness/Fruit of Spirit anchor chart (one per week)

suggestedLibraryBooks.title: National Audubon Society Field Guide to Trees
suggestedLibraryBooks.author: Various

day1_introductionChanges.blocks: Add tree cookie cross sections, cardboard tube trunks, brown/green fabric pieces, bark texture mats
day1_introductionChanges.dramaticPlay: Transform into a garden center/tree nursery: pots, small plants, trowels, watering cans, seed packets, cash register, gardening gloves, aprons
day1_introductionChanges.art: Add leaf rubbings, bark rubbings, earth tones (brown, green, gold, rust), natural materials (acorns, pinecones, dried leaves), watercolors
day1_introductionChanges.library: Add tree book basket (required + suggested books), tree identification cards, nature journals, magnifying glasses
day1_introductionChanges.discovery: Add bark samples, leaf collection, pinecones, acorns, seeds, magnifying glasses, balance scale, nature sorting trays
day1_introductionChanges.sandWater: Add potting soil, small cups for planting, seeds, small shovels — transform into a planting station
day1_introductionChanges.musicMovement: Add nature sounds recordings, tree movement scarves (green/brown), parachute for wind activity
day1_introductionChanges.outdoors: Adopt a tree on the playground for the month — mark it with a ribbon, visit daily

days.focus: Welcome to Exploring Trees — What is a tree?
days.letsThink: Have you ever hugged a tree? (Yes/No graph)
days.primarySkillBuilder: How to Describe a Tree (building anchor chart)
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Nature Journal — My First Tree Drawing
days.outdoorActivity: Adopt Our Tree — greeting and first observation
days.specialNotes: Day 1 — introduce all learning area changes. Tour each area. Introduce Tree Parts poster. Start Wonder Wall. Start Class Tree Journal.
days.continuityNotes: This is Day 1 — no prior lessons to reference. Focus on wonder, curiosity, and belonging.

days.focus: Tree parts — roots, trunk, branches, leaves
days.letsThink: Which part of the tree do you think does the most important job? (bark rubbing displayed)
days.requiredBook: A Tree Is Nice (continued — focus on tree parts)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Root Discovery — celery stalk experiment setup
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Tree Parts Puzzle — label and draw
days.outdoorActivity: Root Hunt — look for exposed roots on the playground
days.continuityNotes: Reference Day 1 How to Describe a Tree chart. Add new vocabulary to chart. Reference Wonder Wall questions about roots.

days.focus: Bark — a tree's skin
days.letsThink: What do you think bark does for a tree? (prediction sticky notes)
days.requiredBook: Tell Me, Tree (Gail Gibbons) or A Tree Is Nice
days.primarySkillBuilder: Bark Rubbings — texture exploration and comparison
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Sorting Bark Samples — Discovery area sorting
days.outdoorActivity: Bark Explorers — examine bark on adopted tree and playground trees
days.continuityNotes: Reference celery experiment started Day 2 — any changes yet? Add bark to How to Describe a Tree chart.

days.focus: Leaves — a tree's food makers
days.letsThink: Why do you think leaves are green? (prediction — 3 choice options displayed)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Leaf Sort and Classify
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Leaf Sun Catchers — art with tissue paper
days.outdoorActivity: Leaf Hunt — collect fallen leaves, compare
days.continuityNotes: Reference celery experiment — show colored water traveling up stem. Connect to how leaves get water. Add to Class Tree Journal.

days.focus: Putting it together — a whole tree
days.letsThink: If a tree could talk, what do you think it would say? (open-ended — children dictate responses on strips)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Build a Tree — collaborative floor puzzle/mural
days.additionalSkillBuilder: My Tree Book — individual mini book (roots, trunk, branches, leaves pages)
days.outdoorActivity: Full Tree Observation — sketch our adopted tree using all week's vocabulary
days.specialNotes: Week 1 reflection — revisit Wonder Wall. What questions have we answered? What new questions do we have?
days.continuityNotes: Celebrate all week's learning. Review How to Describe a Tree chart. Add Week 1 learning to Class Tree Journal.

days.focus: What trees need — sun, water, soil
days.letsThink: Which do you think a tree needs more — sun or water? (balance scale with picture cards)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Planting Our Own Seed — cup garden setup
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Sun, Water, Soil sorting game
days.outdoorActivity: Sunshine Detectives — where does the sun hit our adopted tree?
days.continuityNotes: Introduce Week 2 Fruit of Spirit: Kindness. Connect trees needing care to how we care for each other.

days.focus: Trees give us oxygen and clean air
days.letsThink: What do you think comes out of a leaf? (2 picture choices displayed)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Breathing Like a Tree — wind and air movement experiments
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Clean Air mural — collaborative art
days.outdoorActivity: Deep Breathing under our tree — feel the fresh air
days.continuityNotes: Check on seeds planted Day 6. Any sprouts? Add observations to Class Tree Journal.

days.focus: Trees give us food
days.letsThink: Which of these foods comes from a tree? (display: apple, banana, orange, cherry, walnut, carrot — children sort)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Tree Foods Tasting — sorting and describing
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Graphing our favorites — which tree food do you like best?
days.outdoorActivity: Snack under our tree — eating tree foods outside
fruitfulMoments: Fruit of the Spirit Song (new — connects food + virtue)
days.continuityNotes: Check seeds. Connect Giving Tree story to Kindness — the tree keeps giving. Add to Wonder Wall.

days.focus: Trees give us shelter and shade
days.letsThink: Which animal do you think lives in a tree? (sticky dot voting)
days.requiredBook: A Tree Is Nice — revisit with shelter focus
days.primarySkillBuilder: Leaf Sort and Classify (extended — weight comparison)
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Nature Journal — My Shelter Tree drawing
days.outdoorActivity: Shade Detectives
days.continuityNotes: This is the sample lesson generated earlier — Day 4 of Week 2 in original sample = Day 9 of full exploration. Seeds growing — document progress.

days.focus: Trees give us wood and materials
days.letsThink: Look around our room — what do you see that might have come from a tree? (children place sticky notes on items)
days.requiredBook: The Giving Tree — revisit, focus on what the tree gives
days.primarySkillBuilder: Wood exploration — comparing wood items in the classroom
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Woodworking sensory — sandpaper, different wood textures
days.outdoorActivity: Wood Hunt — find wood items on the playground and school building
days.specialNotes: Week 2 reflection — revisit Wonder Wall. What new questions about how trees help us?
days.continuityNotes: Revisit Giving Tree — the tree gave everything. How do we feel about that? Connect to Kindness. Check seeds — Week 2 close.

days.focus: Trees in different seasons
days.letsThink: What do you think our tree looks like in winter? (4 season pictures — children sequence)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Four Seasons Tree — art and sequencing activity
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Seasons sorting cards
days.outdoorActivity: Our Adopted Tree — what season does it look like now?
days.continuityNotes: Introduce Week 3 Fruit: Faithfulness — trees are faithful, they come back every spring. Connect to God's faithfulness.

days.focus: Animals that live in trees — birds
days.letsThink: What do birds use to build a nest? (display materials — children predict)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Nest Building — using natural materials
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Bird observation journal
days.outdoorActivity: Bird Watch — listen and look near our adopted tree
days.continuityNotes: Check seeds — growth progress. Add bird discoveries to Wonder Wall and Class Tree Journal.

days.focus: Animals that live in trees — insects and small creatures
days.letsThink: Which creature do you think lives in our adopted tree? (class vote with picture cards)
days.requiredBook: Outside Your Window (Nicola Davies) or nature field guide
days.primarySkillBuilder: Bug Hotel — building a small habitat from natural materials
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Creature close-up — magnifying glass exploration
days.outdoorActivity: Creature Hunt — careful, gentle search around our adopted tree
days.continuityNotes: Emphasize gentle observation — we are guests in the tree's habitat. Connect to being faithful caretakers.

days.focus: Tree communities — forests and groves
days.letsThink: What do you think happens when trees grow close together? (prediction with forest photo displayed)
days.requiredBook: An Oak Tree (Andrew Larsen) or The Book Tree
days.primarySkillBuilder: Forest Floor diorama — small group collaborative build
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Sorting living vs. nonliving things in a forest
days.outdoorActivity: Our Tree's Neighbors — observe other trees near our adopted tree
days.continuityNotes: Connect forest community to Circle of Friends — trees help each other, just like we help each other. Add to Class Tree Journal.

days.focus: How trees change over time — a tree's life
days.letsThink: How old do you think the biggest tree you have ever seen was? (guess and record)
days.requiredBook: Be a Tree! — revisit with life cycle focus
days.primarySkillBuilder: Tree Life Cycle sequencing and art
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Counting tree rings — how old is this tree?
days.outdoorActivity: Tree measurement — how tall, how wide around our adopted tree?
days.specialNotes: Week 3 reflection — revisit Wonder Wall. What have we learned about tree communities and change?
days.continuityNotes: Seeds from Day 6 — measure and document growth. Faithfulness theme: the tree keeps growing even when we don't see it.

days.focus: People who care for trees — arborists and foresters
days.letsThink: What do you think a tree doctor does? (job picture cards displayed)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Tree Doctor role play — examining and caring for a tree
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Community helpers — who helps trees in our neighborhood?
days.outdoorActivity: Tree check-up — gentle observation and care of our adopted tree
days.continuityNotes: Introduce Week 4 Fruit: Gentleness — being gentle with trees and with each other. Connect Lorax to stewardship.

days.focus: How we can help trees
days.letsThink: What is one thing WE can do to help trees? (children write or draw on leaves, add to class tree)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Paper saving — why we use both sides, making recycled paper
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Class pledge — how will our Circle of Friends be tree helpers?
days.outdoorActivity: Litter pick — keeping our tree's home clean and healthy
days.continuityNotes: Check seeds — they are now seedlings! Document with photos for Class Tree Journal.

days.focus: Planting a tree — giving back
days.letsThink: If you could plant a tree anywhere in the world, where would you plant it? (map display — children place stickers)
days.primarySkillBuilder: Transplanting our seedlings — moving to larger pots
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Writing to our seedling — dictated letter of care instructions
days.outdoorActivity: Could we plant a tree here? — examining the playground for a good spot
days.continuityNotes: Our seeds are now seedlings — celebrate! Connect transplanting to nurturing relationships. These go home at end of exploration.

days.focus: Trees connect us — family trees and community roots
days.letsThink: Who is in your family tree? (family tree template displayed)
days.requiredBook: The Giving Tree — final revisit, full discussion
days.primarySkillBuilder: My Family Tree — art and dictation project
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Our Circle of Friends Tree — class collaborative tree with each child's leaf
days.outdoorActivity: Farewell visit — draw/paint our adopted tree one final time
days.continuityNotes: Connect family roots to tree roots — we are all connected. Gentleness with our memories, our families, our friends.

days.focus: Celebration — Everything we know about trees
days.letsThink: What is your favorite thing you learned about trees this month? (write on a leaf for our class tree)
days.vocabulary: Review all: trunk, roots, branches, leaves, bark, shelter, shade, habitat, canopy, stewardship
days.requiredBook: Child-chosen favorites from the month — vote for the class favorite
days.primarySkillBuilder: Tree Experts — each child shares one thing they know
days.additionalSkillBuilder: Our Class Tree Journal — final page, reading it together
days.outdoorActivity: Tree celebration — share seedlings to take home, say goodbye to adopted tree
fruitfulMoments: All our favorite Fruitful Moments — children choose
days.specialNotes: Day 20 — closing celebration. Send seedlings home with care instructions. Family letter with all vocabulary and book list. Final Wonder Wall review.
days.continuityNotes: Full reflection on all 20 days. Celebrate growth — theirs AND the seedlings. Close Class Tree Journal.

days.focus: Trees and art — natural materials as creative tools
days.letsThink: What could you make with only things you find in nature?
days.notes: Optional day for extended months or for deepening creative arts

days.focus: Trees in stories and faith — wisdom of trees in scripture
days.letsThink: Have you ever heard a story about a tree in church or at home?
days.notes: Optional day — deeper faith integration, Psalm 1 tree imagery, Zacchaeus, burning bush

days.focus: Trees around the world — different climates, different trees
days.letsThink: What if a tree could grow in the desert? What would it look like?
days.notes: Optional day — cultural diversity extension, global awareness

days.focus: Tree measurement and math — deeper math extension
days.letsThink: How many of us standing hand-to-hand would it take to reach around the biggest tree in the world?
days.notes: Optional day — STEM extension, measurement, estimation, graphing

days.focus: Family Tree Day — families join us
days.letsThink: What do you want to show your family about what we learned?
days.vocabulary: Review — child-chosen words to teach their family
days.notes: Optional day — family engagement event, children as teachers, share Class Tree Journal and seedlings
